_instructions.how_to_add_sentences: Add an object to the 'sentences' array with text, category, and fr (French translation). Categories: greetings, travel, work, social.
_instructions.how_to_add_vocabulary: Add an object to 'vocabulary' with word, pos (noun/verb/adjective/adverb), definition, example, level (a1/a2/b1/b2/c1/c2).
_instructions.how_to_add_quiz_questions: Add an object to 'quiz' with q (question), options (array of 4 strings), correct (index 0-3 of the right answer), and optional explanation.
_instructions.how_to_add_scenarios: Add a key to 'conversation.scenarios'. Each scenario needs opening (first bot message), replies (array of suggested user replies), and responses (object where keys are keywords and 'default' is the fallback).
_instructions.how_to_add_scenario_buttons: Add an object to 'conversation.scenarioButtons' with id (must match scenario key), label, and emoji.
_instructions.vocabulary_levels: a1 = beginner, a2 = elementary, b1 = intermediate, b2 = upper-intermediate, c1 = advanced, c2 = mastery

sentences.text: Could you please repeat that more slowly?
I'd like to make a reservation for two, please.
sentences.fr: Je voudrais faire une réservation pour deux, s'il vous plaît.

sentences.text: It was great to catch up with you!
Could I reschedule our meeting to Thursday?
sentences.fr: Pourrions-nous reporter notre réunion à jeudi ?

sentences.text: Excuse me, where is the nearest subway station?
sentences.fr: Excusez-moi, où est la station de métro la plus proche ?

sentences.text: I'm looking forward to hearing from you.
Nice to meet you, I've heard so much about you!
sentences.fr: Ravi de vous rencontrer, j'ai beaucoup entendu parler de vous !

sentences.text: Could you give me a hand with this?
That's a great point, I hadn't thought of it that way.
sentences.fr: C'est un excellent point, je n'avais pas envisagé cela ainsi.

sentences.text: What would you recommend on the menu?

vocabulary.definition: Fluent and persuasive in speaking or writing.
vocabulary.example: She gave an eloquent speech about climate change.

vocabulary.definition: Feeling or showing thanks for something received.
vocabulary.example: I'm so grateful for your help yesterday.

vocabulary.definition: To work jointly with others on a project.
vocabulary.example: We collaborated on the project for six months.

vocabulary.definition: Able to recover quickly from difficulties.
vocabulary.example: She was resilient in the face of challenges.

vocabulary.definition: To ask for information about something.
vocabulary.example: I'd like to inquire about the job opening.

vocabulary.definition: Having or showing intense interest or excitement.
vocabulary.example: He was enthusiastic about the new project.
There was a substantial improvement in her grades.

vocabulary.definition: To discuss in order to reach an agreement.
vocabulary.example: They negotiated the terms of the contract.

quiz.q: Which sentence uses the correct tense?
options: Yesterday I go to the park.
Yesterday I went to the park.
Yesterday I have gone to the park.
Yesterday I will go to the park.
quiz.explanation: 'Went' is the simple past tense of 'go', used for completed actions in the past.

quiz.q: Choose the correct article: ___ honest person.
quiz.explanation: Use 'an' before words that start with a vowel sound. 'Honest' starts with a silent H, so it sounds like 'onest'.
Eloquent describes someone who speaks or writes in a clear, fluent and persuasive way.

quiz.q: Which is the correct plural of 'tooth'?
quiz.explanation: Tooth has an irregular plural: teeth. Other irregular plurals: foot→feet, goose→geese.

quiz.q: Fill in: She ___ to the store every morning.
quiz.explanation: Third person singular (she/he/it) takes 'goes' in the simple present tense.
Collaborate comes from Latin 'collaborare' — col (together) + laborare (to work).

quiz.q: Choose the right preposition: I'm interested ___ learning English.
quiz.explanation: The correct collocation is 'interested in'. Other fixed prepositions: good at, fond of, afraid of.

quiz.q: What is the past tense of 'bring'?
quiz.explanation: Bring → brought is an irregular verb. Other similar pairs: think→thought, buy→bought, seek→sought.

cafe.opening: Hi! Welcome to The Morning Cup ☕ What can I get for you today?
replies: I'd like a large coffee, please.
Can I get a table for two?

responses.coffee: Great choice! Would you like that black or with milk?
responses.recommend: Our oat milk latte is very popular! Would you like to try one?
responses.tea: Absolutely! We have green, black, and herbal teas. Which do you prefer?
responses.table: Of course! Right this way. Would you like to see the menu?
responses.default: Of course! Anything else I can get for you?

airport.opening: Good morning! May I see your passport and boarding pass, please?
replies: I have an e-ticket, is that okay?

responses.e-ticket: Yes, that's perfectly fine! Can you show me the QR code?
responses.flight: Your flight to London is approximately 7 hours and 40 minutes.
responses.gate: Yes, this is Gate 12B for the 10:45 flight. Boarding begins in 30 minutes.
responses.default: Thank you! Your seat is 24C, by the window. Have a great flight!

job.opening: Thank you for coming in today. Could you tell me a little about yourself?

responses.experience: That's excellent! Can you tell me about a challenge you overcame at work?
responses.passionate: That's great to hear! What specifically draws you to this role?
responses.team: Collaboration is key here. Can you give an example of successful teamwork?
responses.learner: We love that quality! How do you usually approach learning something new?
responses.default: Very interesting! Where do you see yourself in five years?

shop.opening: Welcome! Can I help you find something today?
replies: Do you have this in blue?

responses.gift: Wonderful! Who is it for? We have a lovely gift wrap service too.
responses.blue: Let me check the stockroom for you. One moment, please!
responses.cost: That item is $45, but we have a 20% sale on today!
responses.changing: The changing rooms are at the back, on your left. How many items?
responses.default: Sure thing! Let me know if you need any help.
